methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Heron Power, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Drew Baglino, has raised $140M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public and eight private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were triangulated. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $140M total raised as a baseline proxy, implying a valuation of $266M. The peer-median method benchmarks Heron Power against six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $355M and a range of $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in capital efficiency and market positioning within the climate tech cohort.

Synthesizing both methods produces a valuation range of $226M to $408M, with a median of $310M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables support the upper end. Key uncertainties include the appropriate treatment of Commonwealth Fusion Systems—valued at $5.0B, a substantial outlier—and the extent to which Heron Power's specific technology, burn rate, and commercialization progress align with or diverge from peer characteristics. The wide peer range underscores the difficulty of precise benchmarking in early-stage climate tech.
